name: Neon Kanji Cyberpunk Landing
description: A full-bleed void-black landing page where the headline uses four stacked CSS text-shadows in hot pink and cyan to simulate a neon tube glow, with a keyframe flicker animation that stutters the opacity like a faulty transformer. Vertical Japanese-inspired text runs down the left margin in a narrow column, set in a condensed gothic and reading top-to-bottom. Product images are rendered with a CSS chromatic aberration filter — three layered copies of the image offset by 2px in red, green, and blue channels using mix-blend-mode screen. A full-viewport canvas element behind everything draws scrolling rain-static noise in real time. A scanline overlay sits on top of the entire page at 2px repeating-linear-gradient intervals. Card borders default to transparent but on hover glow hot pink with a 0.4s ease-in transition — like a neon tube warming up from cold gas to full burn. The interface doesn't reference cyberpunk, it IS a neon sign that someone wired up as a website.

vibes.mood: Feels like buying black-market firmware from a rain-soaked street vendor in Kabukichō — every surface is wet and reflective, the vertical kanji crawls down the left edge like a building sign viewed from the sidewalk, the neon flicker catches your peripheral vision, and the card borders warm up on hover like gas ionizing inside a glass tube. The scanlines remind you this is a screen. The chromatic aberration reminds you the screen is lying.
influences: ghost in the shell UI design

meta.audience: Gamers, cyberpunk enthusiasts, and hardware collectors who want a storefront that feels like entering a district — those for whom visual excess is atmosphere, not decoration, and who notice when the hover delay feels like a neon tube warming up
